mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing present equity values and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns is positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate at or below 5.0% signals a tight labor market that increases wage pressure and reduces corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is at or below 5.0%, positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate a broad acceleration in real economic activity that raises corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: MoM change in INDPRO > 0 and MoM change in RSAFS > 0
failure_condition: In months where both INDPRO and RSAFS rise, positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a consumer price inflation rate above 2% forces the Federal Reserve to increase the federal funds rate faster than discounted by markets, raising real discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.0% during tightening cycles, 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative month-over-month growth in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing output and weakening corporate sales, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative MoM changes in INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1200 thousand units signal accelerating residential investment and downstream consumption demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1200, negative 60-day forward returns occur in more than 45% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of declining unemployment and rising payroll employment signals a robust labor market that boosts household income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE MoM change < 0 and PAYEMS MoM change > 0
failure_condition: When UNRATE falls and PAYEMS rises in the same month, positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of rising industrial production indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM change > 0 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Two consecutive months of rising INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day forward returns in more than 45% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.
